name: Cornmeal Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the flour, cornmeal, salt, baking powder, and sugar into a large bowl.
2. Add the shortening and work it in with your fingertips until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Gradually add the milk and stir with a fork until a dough forms.
4. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and lightly knead 10 to 12 times until smooth.
5. Roll the dough out to a thickness of 1/2 inch.
6. Cut into 2-inch rounds.
7. Bake on a baking sheet lightly dusted with cornmeal in a 425 degree oven for 12 to 15 minutes, or until lightly browned.
Note: Formatted by Lynn Thomas. Source: Los Angeles Times 3-23-97. 18 biscuits. Each biscuit: 108 calories; 164 mg sodium; 1 mg cholesterol; 6 grams fat; 12 grams carbohydrates; 2 grams protein; 0.06 gram fiber. Lynn's notes: I made these for Easter diner in 1997 and cut them with a bunny-shaped cookie cutter. They were a delicious accompaniment to the ham.
